description:
You are working as an MLOps engineer on a small research project that tracks experiment-level metadata in two configuration files:

1. A YAML file that keeps a growing list of experiments.
2. A TOML file that maps the same experiment names to their artifact locations.

The project directory tree already exists:

/home/user/mlops  
├── configs  
│   └── experiments.yaml  
├── registry  
│   └── artifacts.toml  
└── logs  

Your goal is to register a brand-new experiment ( resnet50_cifar10 ) in both configuration files and leave an auditable log entry.  
Because this is a simple, one-off operation, the team policy is “**do it with one explicit shell command per file**”; in other words the final solution should not rely on interactive editors such as *vim* or *nano*.  

When you are finished, the following three files must exist exactly as specified (the automated grader does a byte-for-byte comparison, including spaces, newlines and ordering):

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/mlops/configs/experiments.yaml  
Expected YAML content (2-space indentation, newline at EOF):

experiments:
  - id: 1
    name: baseline_cnn
    dataset: mnist
    accuracy: 0.975
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/1
  - id: 2
    name: mobilenet_v2
    dataset: cifar10
    accuracy: 0.855
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/2
  - id: 3
    name: resnet50_cifar10
    dataset: cifar10
    accuracy: 0.912
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/3

Notes:
• The new block for id 3 must appear **after** the existing two blocks.  
• Use exactly the same key order shown above.  
• Each YAML list item starts with two spaces followed by a hyphen.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. /home/user/mlops/registry/artifacts.toml  
Expected TOML content (newline at EOF):

# MLOps experiment artifact registry
[baseline_cnn]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/1"

[mobilenet_v2]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/2"

[resnet50_cifar10]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/3"

Notes:
• Preserve the header comment and blank lines exactly as shown.  
• Append the new table [resnet50_cifar10] at the end.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. /home/user/mlops/logs/experiment_registration.log  
Expected log file (single line, newline at EOF):

2024-05-12T14:30:00Z Experiment resnet50_cifar10 registered

Notes:
• Use this exact ISO-8601 timestamp string (do *not* substitute the current date).  
• If the file already exists, simply overwrite it with the one required line.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Summary of what you need to do
A. Insert the new YAML block (id 3) into experiments.yaml.  
B. Append the new TOML table [resnet50_cifar10] to artifacts.toml.  
C. Create/overwrite the audit log file with the single fixed-timestamp line.  

All three deliverables must match the reference formats above exactly so that an automated “diff” test passes.  
Remember: avoid interactive editors—stick to a direct file-editing shell command for each file.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task starts:

1. /home/user/mlops/configs/experiments.yaml

experiments:
  - id: 1
    name: baseline_cnn
    dataset: mnist
    accuracy: 0.975
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/1
  - id: 2
    name: mobilenet_v2
    dataset: cifar10
    accuracy: 0.855
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/2

2. /home/user/mlops/registry/artifacts.toml

# MLOps experiment artifact registry
[baseline_cnn]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/1"

[mobilenet_v2]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/2"

3. /home/user/mlops/logs  (directory exists but may be empty)

------------------------------------------------------------------
Expected final state after successful completion:

File: /home/user/mlops/configs/experiments.yaml  
Contents (with trailing newline):

experiments:
  - id: 1
    name: baseline_cnn
    dataset: mnist
    accuracy: 0.975
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/1
  - id: 2
    name: mobilenet_v2
    dataset: cifar10
    accuracy: 0.855
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/2
  - id: 3
    name: resnet50_cifar10
    dataset: cifar10
    accuracy: 0.912
    artifact_uri: s3://ml-bucket/experiments/3

File: /home/user/mlops/registry/artifacts.toml  
Contents (with trailing newline):

# MLOps experiment artifact registry
[baseline_cnn]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/1"

[mobilenet_v2]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/2"

[resnet50_cifar10]
uri = "s3://ml-bucket/experiments/3"

File: /home/user/mlops/logs/experiment_registration.log  
Contents (with trailing newline):

2024-05-12T14:30:00Z Experiment resnet50_cifar10 registered
name: task_000000_eeb1a6a7
